heading: Withholding
label: 1234d Withholding

children.heading: Discretionary authority over further payments under applicable program
children.label: a Discretionary authority over further payments under applicable program
children.text: In accordance with , the Secretary may withhold from a recipient, in whole or in part, further payments (including payments for administrative costs) under an applicable program.section 1234c of this title
the factual and legal basis for the Secretary’s belief that the recipient has failed to comply substantially with a requirement of law; and
an opportunity for a hearing to be held on a date at least 30 days after the notification has been sent to the recipient.

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: c Hearing
children.text: The hearing shall be held before the Office and shall be conducted in accordance with the rules prescribed pursuant to subsections (f) and (g) of .section 1234 of this title

children.heading: Suspension of payments, authorities, etc.
children.label: d Suspension of payments, authorities, etc.
children.text: Pending the outcome of any hearing under this section, the Secretary may suspend payments to a recipient, suspend the authority of the recipient to obligate Federal funds, or both, after such recipient has been given reasonable notice and an opportunity to show cause why future payments or authority to obligate Federal funds should not be suspended.

children.heading: Findings of fact
children.label: e Findings of fact
children.text: Upon review of a decision of the Office by the Secretary, the findings of fact by the Office, if supported by substantial evidence, shall be conclusive. However, the Secretary, for good cause shown, may remand the case to the Office to take further evidence, and the Office may thereupon make new or modified findings of fact and may modify its previous action. Such new or modified findings of fact shall likewise be conclusive if supported by substantial evidence.

modifies or sets aside the decision, in whole or in part, in which case the decision of the Secretary shall become final agency action when the recipient receives written notice of the Secretary’s action; or
remands the decision of the Office.
